questions.q: In which Indian state is Jaisalmer Fort located?
questions.explanation: Jaisalmer Fort stands in the city of Jaisalmer in the western Indian state of Rajasthan.

questions.q: What is the popular nickname of Jaisalmer Fort?
questions.explanation: Built of yellow sandstone that glows golden at sunset, it is famously called the 'Golden Fort' or 'Sonar Quila'.

questions.q: From which desert does Jaisalmer Fort rise?
questions.explanation: Jaisalmer Fort rises from the sands of the Thar Desert in western Rajasthan.

questions.q: What type of stone gives Jaisalmer Fort its golden colour?
questions.explanation: The fort is built of local yellow sandstone, which turns a honey-gold hue in the sunlight.

questions.q: What is the local name 'Sonar Quila' that refers to Jaisalmer Fort?
questions.explanation: 'Sonar Quila' literally means 'Golden Fort', a name inspired by the fort's glowing sandstone.

questions.q: What is especially unusual about Jaisalmer Fort compared with most Indian forts?
options: It is still inhabited by residents
questions.explanation: Jaisalmer Fort is one of the world's few 'living forts', still home to thousands of residents within its walls.

questions.q: Which community originally founded and ruled Jaisalmer Fort?
questions.explanation: The fort was founded by Rajput rulers, specifically the Bhati Rajput clan of Jaisalmer.

questions.q: What kind of structure is Jaisalmer Fort?
options: A hilltop fort and walled town
questions.explanation: It is a massive hilltop fort that encloses a walled town of palaces, temples, havelis and homes.

questions.q: On which prominent landform does Jaisalmer Fort sit?
questions.explanation: The fort crowns Trikuta Hill, a triangular ridge that gives it a commanding view over the desert.

questions.q: Jaisalmer Fort is recognised as part of which UNESCO listing?
questions.explanation: Jaisalmer Fort is one of the six forts inscribed under the UNESCO World Heritage Site 'Hill Forts of Rajasthan'.

questions.q: Who is traditionally credited with founding Jaisalmer Fort in 1156 CE?
questions.explanation: The Bhati Rajput ruler Rawal Jaisal is credited with founding the fort and the city of Jaisalmer in 1156 CE.

questions.q: After whom is the city and fort of Jaisalmer named?
questions.explanation: Both the fort and the city take their name from their founder, the Bhati Rajput ruler Rawal Jaisal.

questions.q: Roughly in which century was Jaisalmer Fort founded?
questions.explanation: The fort was founded in 1156 CE, placing its origins in the 12th century.

questions.q: Which Rajput clan ruled Jaisalmer and built its fort?
questions.explanation: Jaisalmer was the seat of the Bhati Rajputs, who founded and ruled the Golden Fort for centuries.

questions.q: What largely made medieval Jaisalmer prosperous, supporting the fort and its havelis?
options: Caravan trade routes across the desert
questions.explanation: Jaisalmer thrived on the lucrative caravan trade routes linking India with Central Asia and the Middle East.

questions.q: Which religion's temples are notably found inside Jaisalmer Fort?
questions.explanation: The fort contains a celebrated group of intricately carved Jain temples dating from the medieval period.

questions.q: Approximately how many bastions (towers) does the outer wall of Jaisalmer Fort have?
questions.explanation: The fort's fortifications are reinforced by around 99 bastions strung along its outer walls.

questions.q: What are the ornately carved mansions found in and around Jaisalmer commonly called?
questions.explanation: Jaisalmer is famous for its 'havelis', richly carved sandstone merchant mansions such as Patwon Ki Haveli.

questions.q: How many fortified gateways (pols) traditionally guard the approach into Jaisalmer Fort?
questions.explanation: The winding entrance to the fort passes through a series of four massive gateways known as pols.

questions.q: Which deity is the principal subject of the main Jain temples within Jaisalmer Fort?
questions.explanation: The fort's Jain temples are dedicated to the Tirthankaras, the revered enlightened teachers of Jainism.

questions.q: Which famous Indian filmmaker set a celebrated film in Jaisalmer, popularising the name 'Sonar Kella'?
questions.explanation: Satyajit Ray's 1974 film 'Sonar Kella' (The Golden Fortress) was set in Jaisalmer and made the fort famous.

questions.q: What is a major modern conservation threat to Jaisalmer Fort's foundations?
questions.explanation: Increased water use and poor drainage have caused seepage that weakens the fort's sandstone foundations.

questions.q: Which prominent royal residence stands at the entrance area inside Jaisalmer Fort?
questions.explanation: The seven-storeyed Raj Mahal, the palace of Jaisalmer's rulers, overlooks the main square inside the fort.

questions.q: The Bhati Rajputs of Jaisalmer traced their lineage to which legendary dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Bhatis claimed descent from the Chandravanshi or lunar dynasty, tracing ancestry to Lord Krishna's line.

questions.q: Jaisalmer Fort is regarded as one of the largest fortifications of its kind in the world. Which description fits?
options: One of the smallest desert forts
Among the very largest fully inhabited forts
An entirely ruined and abandoned fort
questions.explanation: It ranks among the largest forts in the world and is one of the very few that remains fully inhabited.

questions.q: Approximately how high do the walls of Jaisalmer Fort rise above the surrounding terrain on Trikuta Hill?
questions.explanation: The fort rises roughly 75 metres above the town below atop Trikuta Hill, dominating the desert skyline.

questions.q: Which traditional defensive feature was historically used against attackers climbing the fort walls?
options: Boiling oil poured from the ramparts
questions.explanation: Defenders historically poured boiling oil and rolled cannonballs down on attackers from the high ramparts.

questions.q: In which year was Jaisalmer Fort inscribed as part of the UNESCO 'Hill Forts of Rajasthan' World Heritage Site?
questions.explanation: The Hill Forts of Rajasthan, including Jaisalmer Fort, were inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List in 2013.

questions.q: Why does Jaisalmer Fort appear to change colour through the day?
options: The yellow sandstone shifts from tawny to honey-gold with the sun
Coloured lights are projected on it
It is covered in tinted glass
questions.explanation: The fort's yellow sandstone shifts from a tawny lion colour by day to a glowing honey-gold at sunset.

questions.q: Besides the Bhati Rajputs, how did Jaisalmer's status change after India's independence in 1947?
options: The princely state was merged into independent India and later into Rajasthan
It was ceded to a foreign power
questions.explanation: The princely state of Jaisalmer acceded to India after 1947 and was integrated into the state of Rajasthan.
